basic_details.job_title: NPCIL KKNPP Stipendiary Trainee Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online for 255 Posts
basic_details.organization_name: Nuclear Power Corporation of India Limited (NPCIL), Kudankulam Nuclear Power Project (KKNPP), Tirunelveli District, Tamil Nadu – 627106
basic_details.department: Nuclear Power Corporation of India Limited (NPCIL) - under Department of Atomic Energy, Government of India
basic_details.short_information: NPCIL KKNPP Stipendiary Trainee Technician Recruitment 2026: Get the latest Nuclear Power Corporation of India Limited (NPCIL), Kudankulam Nuclear Power Project (KKNPP) recruitment notification 2026 for 255 Category-II Stipendiary Trainee/Technician (ST/TN) vacancies across 8 trades. The online application process starts on 8th June 2026 and the last date to apply online is 29th June 2026. Find NPCIL vacancies on the official website npcilcareers.co.in. You can also get Free Job Alert of "Upcoming NPCIL KKNPP Stipendiary Trainee Recruitment 2026" notifications for freshers and experienced cand

important_dates.online examination date: To be notified on the official NPCIL careers website

application_fee.details: Application fee is non-refundable and must be paid online through the payment gateway integrated with the application form (debit card / credit card / net banking / UPI). The application number will be generated only after successful payment of the fee. Applications without payment of the prescribed fee will be rejected. Female candidates, SC, ST, PwBD, Ex-Servicemen, Dependents of Defence Personnel Killed in Action (DODPKIA), and NPCIL employees are exempted from payment of application fee.

age_limit.details: Candidates must be between 18 and 24 years of age as on 29-06-2026 (born on or after 29-06-2002 and on or before 29-06-2008). Age relaxation is available for various categories. In case a candidate is eligible for age relaxation under more than one category, the relaxations will be on a cumulative basis subject to a maximum of 56 years. There is no age limit for NPCIL employees who otherwise fulfill the prescribed criteria. | Physical Standards | Note: Physical standards are relaxable if the candidate is otherwise medically fit.

qualification.details: Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on the closing date of online application (29-06-2026). All qualifications must be from a recognized University or Institution. Candidates awaiting results of their qualifying examination are not eligible to apply. English must have been studied as one of the subjects at SSC (10th) level. One year of apprenticeship training after completion of a 1-year ITI course will be considered as one year of experience; period of internship will not be counted as experience. The National Apprenticeship Certificate (NAC) for a training duration of 3 years will not be accepted where the essential qualification is a 2-year ITI certificate. | Educational Qualification

salary_details.details: Apply Online: Click here Official Notification PDF: Click here Official Website: Click here Join Arattai Channel: Click Here Join Telegram Channel: Click Here Join WhatsApp Channel: Click Here Sarkari Result: Click Here Download Mobile App: Click Here
salary_details.pay_scale: Year 1: Rs. 20,000/- per month | Year 2: Rs. 22,000/- per month | Book Allowance: Rs. 3,000/- (one time)
salary_details.post-training absorption post: Technician/B - Pay Level 3 (Rs. 21,700/-); Approx. Monthly Emoluments: Rs. 34,720/- (Pay + DA @ 60%)
salary_details.minimum marks for absorption: Trainees scoring less than 60% during training will NOT be absorbed
salary_details.training bond amount: Rs. 5,07,000/- (Indemnity bond / bank guarantee for 5 years)
salary_details.bond breach consequence: Amount repayable equivalent to total stipend plus book allowance actually received
salary_details.total questions / maximum marks: 50 MCQs / 150 Marks (3 marks per correct answer)
salary_details.subject-wise breakup: Mathematics: 20 Questions | Science: 20 Questions | General Awareness: 10 Questions
salary_details.minimum qualifying marks: UR: 40% | SC / ST / OBC (NCL) / EWS / PwBD: 30%
salary_details.nature: Screening exam - common question paper for all disciplines
salary_details.merit list: Prepared based on Stage-2 Advanced Test scores only. Separate merit list for each PwBD disability category.
salary_details.step 1: Lower negative marks in Stage-2 Advanced Test placed higher
salary_details.step 2: Higher marks in Stage-1 Preliminary Test placed higher
salary_details.step 3: Lower negative marks in Stage-1 Preliminary Test placed higher
salary_details.step 4: Higher positive marks in Mathematics in Stage-1 placed higher
salary_details.step 5: Higher positive marks in Science in Stage-1 placed higher

faq.question: Q1. What is the application period and eligibility cut-off date for NPCIL KKNPP Stipendiary Trainee Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The online application portal opens on 8th June 2026 at 10:00 AM and the last date for application submission and fee payment is 29th June 2026 up to 4:00 PM. The crucial date for determining age and all eligibility criteria is 29th June 2026, which is the closing date of application submission. Candidates must ensure all their qualifications are completed by this date, as those awaiting results are not eligible to apply.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are available and what is the trade-wise distribution?
faq.answer: A total of 255 vacancies (208 current + 47 backlog) are available across 8 trades. The trade-wise distribution is: Plant Operator - 136 posts, Electrician - 47 posts, Fitter - 30 posts, Electronics Mechanic - 15 posts, Instrumentation Mechanic - 15 posts, Turner - 5 posts, Machinist - 5 posts, and Welder - 2 posts. Out of the total 255 posts, 11 posts are horizontally reserved for Persons with Benchmark Disabilities (PwBD) across different disability categories.

faq.question: Q3. What is the educational qualification required for Plant Operator and other technical trades?
faq.answer: For Plant Operator positions, candidates must have passed HSC (10+2) or ISC with Science stream (Physics, Chemistry, and Mathematics) with minimum 50% marks in aggregate. For Electronics Mechanic, Instrumentation Mechanic, Electrician, Fitter, Turner, Machinist, and Welder trades, candidates need SSC (10th) with minimum 50% marks in Science subjects and Mathematics individually, plus a 2-year ITI certificate in the relevant trade. For trades where ITI duration is less than 2 years, 1 year of relevant working experience after course completion is mandatory. English must have been studied at SSC (10th) level for all trades.

faq.question: Q4. Can candidates who are awaiting their ITI or 12th results apply for this recruitment?
faq.answer: No, candidates awaiting results of their qualifying examination are NOT eligible to apply for this recruitment. All qualifications - whether HSC (10+2) for Plant Operator or SSC (10th) + ITI for other trades - must be completed by the closing date of application (29th June 2026). This is a strict eligibility criterion and only candidates who have already received their final results and certificates can submit applications.

faq.question: Q5. What is the age limit and what relaxations are available for different categories?
faq.answer: The age limit is 18 to 24 years as on 29th June 2026, meaning candidates must be born between 29-06-2002 and 29-06-2008. Age relaxation is provided as follows: OBC (NCL) - 3 years (max 27 years), SC/ST - 5 years (max 29 years), PwBD-UR/EWS - 10 years (max 34 years), PwBD-OBC - 13 years (max 37 years), PwBD-SC/ST - 15 years (max 39 years). Ex-Servicemen get 3 years relaxation after deduction of military service from actual age. If a candidate is eligible for relaxation under more than one category, relaxations will be on a cumulative basis subject to maximum 56 years.

faq.question: Q6. What is the stipend during training and salary after absorption?
faq.answer: During the 2-year training period, selected candidates will receive a monthly stipend of Rs. 20,000 in Year 1 and Rs. 22,000 in Year 2, along with a one-time book allowance of Rs. 3,000. After successful completion of training with minimum 60% marks, candidates will be absorbed as Technician/B in Pay Level 3 with a basic pay of Rs. 21,700. The approximate monthly emoluments including Dearness Allowance (at 60%) will be around Rs. 34,720.

faq.question: Q7. What is the complete selection process and examination pattern?
faq.answer: The selection consists of three stages conducted on the same day. Stage 1 (Preliminary Test) is a 60-minute screening exam with 50 MCQs for 150 marks covering Mathematics (20 questions), Science (20 questions), and General Awareness (10 questions), with minimum qualifying marks of 40% for UR and 30% for reserved categories. Stage 2 (Advanced Test) is a 120-minute merit exam with 50 MCQs for 150 marks based on HSC level for Plant Operator or ITI trade syllabus for other trades, with minimum qualifying marks of 30% for UR and 20% for reserved categories. Both tests have 3 marks per correct answer and negative marking of 1 mark per wrong answer. Stage 3 involves Document Verification for shortlisted candidates (not exceeding 3 times the vacancies per trade). The final merit list is prepared based solely on Stage-2 Advanced Test scores.

faq.question: Q8. What is the application fee and who is exempted from payment?
faq.answer: Male candidates belonging to General (UR), EWS, and OBC categories must pay a non-refundable application fee of Rs. 100 through online payment gateway (debit card/credit card/net banking/UPI). Female candidates, SC, ST, PwBD, Ex-Servicemen, Dependents of Defence Personnel Killed in Action (DODPKIA), and NPCIL employees are completely exempted from payment of application fee. The application number will be generated only after successful payment of fee (or fee exemption).
